title: People & Hospitality
blurb: Hotels, ryokan, onsen, gifts. These phrases go a long way socially.

phrases.use: First thing at the front desk. Add 'yoyaku shiteimasu' (I have a reservation) below.
Add your name: 'Smith to iu yoyaku desu' (a reservation under Smith).
Hotel check-in, restaurant reservation, anywhere a name is needed. Drop in your name.
phrases.note: Use the anglicized form most hotels expect: 'Sumisu desu' (Smith).

phrases.use: Before check-in time, after checkout — most hotels/ryokan offer free luggage holding.
Standard hotel checkout is 10 or 11 am. Ryokan often earlier. Late checkout may cost extra.

phrases.en: I want to go in the onsen.
phrases.use: Confirm at a ryokan front desk. Often restricted to check-in / 22:00 ish hours.
Some public baths require bringing your own towel (especially free-standing outdoor ones).
Many onsen, public baths, sento, ryokan, and pools refuse entry for visible tattoos. Always ask before entering.
phrases.note: EE-reh-zoo-mee. Conservative answer = stay covered (rash guards, cover-up stickers).

phrases.en: Could you take a photo of me?
phrases.use: Hand your phone to a passerby / shop staff. Smiling + bowing slightly is appreciated.
At a shop or a tourist info center. Often they have a curated 'best for visitors' section.
Point + say it. Works at any shop.
Most department stores have a free wrapping counter; small shops vary.
A compliment that lands with anyone — host, restaurant, onsen manager, taxi driver.
The closing line when leaving a ryokan, homestay, or any place where you were genuinely hosted. Has real weight.
phrases.note: Oh-SEH-wah nee nah-ree-mah-SH-TA. Worth saying out loud.

phrases.en: Pardon me / Excuse me (entering or leaving)
phrases.use: When entering or leaving rooms, especially in traditional inns or other people's homes. Highly cultured.

phrases.en: Should I take off my shoes?
phrases.use: In temples, ryokan, traditional restaurants, in people's homes. Always look at the entry area first.
phrases.note: Look for a sunken area (genkan) and shoe racks. If present: yes.
